unsupported_implied.text: Error messages and batch validation feedback shall be human-readable and actionable (no opaque codes); bulk operations that partially fail must present clear, user-friendly warnings and guidance to fix issues.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Yes—make the error messages human-readable. No mystery codes.
unsupported_implied.reason: The source explicitly demands human-readable error messages and clear feedback for bulk uploads/partial failures. The gold set requires basic data integrity checking but does not require the user-facing quality of messages or explicit handling/clear reporting of partial failures in batch operations.

unsupported_implied.text: The system shall record and retain an audit trail that captures not only who changed data but why a change was made (e.g., reason/justification for moving a student or adjusting a part-time sequence).
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Part-time students complicate everything. We need to manage their progress by adjusting sequence and timelines, and keep an audit trail so we know why we moved them.
unsupported_implied.reason: While the gold set requires a printable system log and general logging, the source explicitly requests capturing the reason/justification for changes (the 'why'). The gold requirements do not specify that log entries must include the rationale for edits, so this is a new, significant audit/capture requirement.

unsupported_implied.text: Provide short training artifacts (e.g., a quick reference guide) as part of deliverables so staff become productive within one day of training.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: One day of training and maybe a quick reference, then productive.
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold set includes training time and usability targets but does not mandate producing concise training materials or a quick reference guide as an explicit deliverable. The source requests a quick reference to support the one-day training goal, which is a distinct documentation/delivery requirement.
